content: Blood Relations — Ravi's father is Mohan's only son. How is Mohan related to Ravi?
explanation: Mohan's only son is Ravi's father. So Mohan is Ravi's grandfather.

content: Blood Relations — A is the mother of B. B is the sister of C. D is the son of C. How is A related to D?
explanation: A is B's mother. B and C are siblings. D is C's child. So A is D's grandmother.

content: Blood Relations — X and Y are brothers. Z is the father of X. W is the son of Y. How is W related to Z?
explanation: Z is X's father. X and Y are brothers so Z is also Y's father. W is Y's son → W is Z's grandson.

content: Blood Relations — A woman says 'He is the son of the only son of my grandfather.' Who is he to her?
explanation: Her grandfather's only son is her father. His son is her brother.

content: Blood Relations — M's father's sister's husband is N. How is N related to M?
explanation: M's father's sister is M's paternal aunt. Her husband is M's paternal uncle.

content: Blood Relations — Seema introduced a boy as the son of the daughter of the father of her uncle. The boy is Seema's?
explanation: Uncle's father = grandfather. Grandfather's daughter = Seema's mother. Son of Seema's mother = Seema's brother.

content: Blood Relations — If Neha is the daughter of Anita and Anita is the sister of Suresh, then Suresh is Neha's?
explanation: Anita is Neha's mother. Suresh is Anita's brother, so Suresh is Neha's maternal uncle.

content: Blood Relations — John introduced a man saying 'He is the husband of the granddaughter of my father's wife.' How is the man related to John?
explanation: John's father's wife = John's mother. Her granddaughter = John's daughter. Her husband = John's son-in-law.

content: Blood Relations — A + B means A is father of B. A − B means A is wife of B. If P + Q − R, what is P to R?
explanation: P+Q: P is father of Q. Q−R: Q is wife of R (R is Q's husband). P is Q's father → P is R's father-in-law.

content: Blood Relations — A's mother is the sister of B's father. A and B are?
explanation: A's mother and B's father are siblings, so A and B share a grandparent — they are cousins.

content: Blood Relations — In a portrait, Raj says 'Her mother is the wife of my father's son. I have no siblings.' Who is in the portrait?
explanation: No siblings → my father's son is Raj. Raj's wife is her mother → she is Raj's daughter.

content: Blood Relations — A is B's sister. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
explanation: C is B's mother and also A's mother (A is B's sister). D is C's father = A's grandfather. A is D's granddaughter.

content: Blood Relations — Priya is Shyam's daughter. Shyam and Gopal are brothers. Gopal's mother is Kamla. How is Priya related to Kamla?
explanation: Kamla is Gopal and Shyam's mother. Priya is Shyam's daughter, so Priya is Kamla's granddaughter.

content: Blood Relations — If A is brother of B, B is sister of C, C is father of D, then how is A related to D?
explanation: A, B, C are all siblings. C is D's father. A is D's uncle.

content: Blood Relations — Pointing to a man, a lady said 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' The man is the lady's?
explanation: Only daughter of the lady's mother is the lady herself. So the man's mother is the lady → he is her son.

content: Blood Relations — Deepak's mother is the sister of Rahul's father. Rahul's father has a son Vivek. How is Deepak related to Vivek?
explanation: Deepak's mother and Vivek's father (Rahul's father) are siblings → Deepak and Vivek are cousins.

content: Blood Relations — A is the wife of B. C is the son of A. D is the father of B. E is the son of D. How is C related to E?
explanation: C is B's son. E is D's son; D is B's father → E is B's brother. C (B's son) and E (B's brother) are cousins.

content: Blood Relations — My mother's brother's only sister's mother is?
explanation: Mother's brother's only sister = my mother. My mother's mother = my grandmother.

content: Blood Relations — P is the father of Q. R is the daughter of S. S is the wife of Q. How is P related to R?
explanation: P is Q's father. S is Q's wife. R is S's daughter = Q's daughter. P is R's grandfather.

content: Blood Relations — If X$Y means X is mother of Y, X@Y means X is husband of Y, X#Y means X is daughter of Y, then what does A@B$C#D mean?
explanation: A@B: A is husband of B. B$C: B is mother of C → A is father of C. C#D: C is daughter of D → D is C's parent. Since B is C's mother and A is C's father, D here represents A's parent → A is D's son, and C is A's child, making A grandfather in the symbolic chain.
